subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Regions R and S — Sum of Areas, Square Cross-Section Volume & Rate of Vertical Distance
stimulus: Let $f$ and $g$ be the functions defined by $f(x) = 1 + x + e^{x^2 - 2x}$ and $g(x) = x^4 - 6.5x^2 + 6x + 2$. Let $R$ and $S$ be the two regions enclosed by the graphs of $f$ and $g$ shown in the figure.

parts.prompt: Find the sum of the areas of regions $R$ and $S$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct limits: intersection points at $x = 0$, $x \approx 1.033$, and $x = 2$
1 pt: Correct integrand for region $R$: $g(x) - f(x)$; correct integrand for region $S$: $f(x) - g(x)$
1 pt: Correct integrals: $\int_0^{1.033} [g(x) - f(x)]\, dx + \int_{1.033}^2 [f(x) - g(x)]\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Intersections at $(0, 2)$, $(2, 4)$, and $(A, B) \approx (1.033, 2.401)$. Area $= \int_0^A [g-f]\, dx + \int_A^2 [f-g]\, dx \approx 0.997 + 1.007 = 2.004$.

parts.prompt: Region $S$ is the base of a solid whose cross sections perpendicular to the $x$-axis are squares. Find the volume of the solid.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integrand: $[f(x) - g(x)]^2$ (square cross sections with side $= f(x) - g(x)$)

parts.prompt: Let $h$ be the vertical distance between the graphs of $f$ and $g$ in region $S$. Find the rate at which $h$ changes with respect to $x$ when $x = 1.8$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Recognizes $h(x) = f(x) - g(x)$; takes derivative: $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $h(x) = f(x) - g(x)$; $h'(1.8) = f'(1.8) - g'(1.8) \approx -3.812$.
